title: CMMC Compliance Assessment

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for CMMC.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CMMC unknown is enforced — Splunk UC-10.12.40: CMMC Compliance Assessment.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-10.12.40' running on sourcetype="o365:audit", sourcetype="dlp:cui", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=cui sourcetype="o365:audit" Workload=SharePoint
| search CUI_label="true" AND SharingScope="Anyone"
| stats count by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId
| sort -count
description: Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.
value: Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We track DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3 — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft 365 GCC High, endpoint DLP.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="o365:audit"`, `sourcetype="dlp:cui"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Encrypt CUI at rest; block personal email domains; SSP alignment to CMMC domains.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cui sourcetype="o365:audit" Workload=SharePoint
| search CUI_label="true" AND SharingScope="Anyone"
| stats count by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMMC Compliance Assessment** — Tracks DFARS 7012 logging, FCI/CUI handling, and practice maturity for CMMC L2/L3.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="o365:audit"`, `sourcetype="dlp:cui"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft 365 GCC High, endpoint DLP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cui; **sourcetype**: o365:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cui, sourcetype="o365:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserId, SiteUrl, ObjectId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (policy violations), Bar chart (practices), Heatmap (domain × status).
